contradictions.claim_text: Bilateral agreements with India, Morocco and Brazil define the recruiting route.
contradictions.resolution: Keep the country framing narrow. The public nursing route is Triple Win with India (Kerala and Telangana), Indonesia, the Philippines and Tunisia; Brazil and Morocco appear on other programmes and should not be marketed as the defining nursing route.
contradictions.why_it_matters: The legacy country list collapses distinct German migration programmes into one nursing-sales story and would misroute targeting.

contradictions.claim_text: 300K+ foreign nurses already work in Germany (17.8%).
contradictions.resolution: Use the dated official numbers instead: 270,000 foreign-national qualified nurses and 16% in 2023, plus an 18% share in nursing professions in 2025.
contradictions.why_it_matters: Inflated, timeless workforce copy weakens credibility and undermines the fail-closed standard.

falsification_searches.query: site:make-it-in-germany.com "Triple Win" nursing India Indonesia Philippines Tunisia
falsification_searches.result: The official nursing route page names the actual Triple Win nursing countries and does not support a Morocco/Brazil nursing-route claim.

falsification_searches.query: site:make-it-in-germany.com Brazil India Vietnam Morocco recruiting skilled workers
falsification_searches.result: Brazil and Morocco appear on other public projects such as Hand in Hand and THAMM Plus, which confirms that the legacy country list conflated separate programmes.
The current BA pages support 270,000 and 16% in 2023, then 18% in 2025; they do not support 300K+ and 17.8% as one safe figure.

falsification_searches.query: site:bamf.de Deutsch fuer den Beruf Gesundheitswesen Fachsprachpruefungen
falsification_searches.result: BAMF proves a real occupational-language support route for healthcare recognition, but not product-specific eligibility for B1.
